message:
🌱 Add fluent-bit: Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s mission (#1804)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "fluent-bit-3905-fluent-bit-container-crashes-with-exit-code-136-on-k8s-logging-t",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "fluent-bit: Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s logging to AWS ES ",
+    "description": "Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s logging to AWS ES . This issue affects 9+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify fluent-bit troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your fluent-bit deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review fluent-bit configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
I'm new to fluent-bit and using the the provided example yaml files with k8s 1.19.12 and AWS ES 7.9.  No customi"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136 on k8s…",
+        "description": "I modified re-allocation logic #3788 
The patch tries to divide by `converted_size`.
If `converted_size` is 0, it causes SIGFPE and fluent-bit exits code 136.

----
Enter `[N/A]` in the box, if an item is not applicable to your change.

Before we can approve your change; please submit the following
```yaml
[INPUT]
    Name tail
    Path a.log
    Read_From_Head true

[OUTPUT]
    Name es
    Match *
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Fluent-bit container crashes with exit code 136… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -n fluent-bit --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I modified re-allocation logic #3788 
The patch tries to divide by `converted_size`.
If `converted_size` is 0, it causes SIGFPE and fluent-bit exits code 136.

----
Enter `[N/A]` in the box, if an item is not applicable to your change.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "[INPUT]
    Name tail
    Path a.log
    Read_From_Head true

[OUTPUT]
    Name es
    Match *",
+        "$ ../bin/fluent-bit -c a.conf -vvv
Fluent Bit v1.9.0
* Copyright (C) 2019-2021 The Fluent Bit Authors
* Copyright (C) 2015-2018 Treasure Data
* Fluent Bit is a CNCF sub-project under the umbrella of Fluentd
* https://fluentbit.io

[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info]  grace          | 5 seconds
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info]  daemon         | 0
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info] ___________

[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info]      tail
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info] ___________
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info]  filters:
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info] ___________
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info]  outputs:
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ info]      es.0
[2021/08/09 13:22:13] [ in",
+        "$ valgrind --leak-check=full ../bin/fluent-bit -c a.conf
==48548== Memcheck, a memory error detector

[2021/08"

+    }
+  },
